title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer identifies that Deployment, Service, and CronJob reconcile loops all compare desired specs against existing specs without normalizing API-server-applied defaults (e.g., `ProgressDeadlineSeconds`, `Strategy`, `ServiceType`, `ConcurrencyPolicy`, etc.), causing `DeepEqual` to always return false and triggering spurious updates on every reconcile — an infinite update loop in production. The fix is to add normalization functions (similar to the existing `PodSpecDefaults`) that fill in these well-known API-server defaults on the desired spec before comparison, for each resource type.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Created `normalize.DeploymentSpecDefaults` in `internal/common/normalize/deployment.go` that fills in `RevisionHistoryLimit` (10), `ProgressDeadlineSeconds` (600), `Strategy.Type` (RollingUpdate), and `Strategy.RollingUpdate` (25%/25%) — with a guard that only sets `RollingUpdate` when the strategy type is `RollingUpdateDeploymentStrategyType`. Updated `EnsureDeployment` in `deployment.go` to normalize both desired and existing specs via `DeepCopy` before comparison (lines 49-53). Added tests in `deployment_test.go` verifying defaults are applied and explicit values (including Recreate strategy) are preserved.

code_comments.body:
**Service-spec-level API defaults not normalized — spurious updates on every reconcile**

`apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, newSpec)` compares the full `ServiceSpec` without normalizing Service-level API-server defaults. When a caller constructs a `Service` without setting the following fields, the stored `existing.Spec` will have the API-server-applied defaults while `newSpec` retains zero/empty values:

| Field | API-server default |
|---|---|
| `Type` | `"ClusterIP"` |
| `SessionAffinity` | `"None"` |
| `InternalTrafficPolicy` | `"Cluster"` |
| `IPFamilyPolicy` | `"SingleStack"` |

For example: a caller that omits `svc.Spec.Type` will have `newSpec.Type == ""` but `existing.Spec.Type == "ClusterIP"`. Since `"" != "ClusterIP"` under `reflect.DeepEqual`, `DeepEqual` returns `false` and `c.Update` is called on every reconcile. Services are among the most commonly created resources, and callers almost never explicitly set `SessionAffinity` or `InternalTrafficPolicy`.

The fix is to normalize service-level defaults before comparison:

```go
if newSpec.Type == "" {
    newSpec.Type = corev1.ServiceTypeClusterIP
}
if newSpec.SessionAffinity == "" {
    newSpec.SessionAffinity = corev1.ServiceAffinityNone
}
if newSpec.InternalTrafficPolicy == nil {
    local := corev1.ServiceInternalTrafficPolicyCluster
    newSpec.InternalTrafficPolicy = &local
}
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, newSpec)` compares the full `ServiceSpec` without normalizing Service-level API-server defaults. When a caller constructs a `Service` without setting the following fields, the stored `existing.Spec` will have the API-server-applied defaults while `newSpec` retains zero/empty values:

| Field | API-server default |
|---|---|
| `Type` | `"ClusterIP"` |
| `SessionAffinity` | `"None"` |
| `InternalTrafficPolicy` | `"Cluster"` |
| `IPFamilyPolicy` | `"SingleStack"` |

For example: a caller that omits `svc.Spec.Type` will have `newSpec.Type == ""` but `existing.Spec.Type == "ClusterIP"`. Since `"" != "ClusterIP"` under `reflect.DeepEqual`, `DeepEqual` returns `false` and `c.Update` is called on every reconcile. Services are among the most commonly created resources, and callers almost never explicitly set `SessionAffinity` or `InternalTrafficPolicy`.

The fix is to normalize service-level defaults before comparison:

```go
if newSpec.Type == "" {
    newSpec.Type = corev1.ServiceTypeClusterIP
}
if newSpec.SessionAffinity == "" {
    newSpec.SessionAffinity = corev1.ServiceAffinityNone
}
if newSpec.InternalTrafficPolicy == nil {
    local := corev1.ServiceInternalTrafficPolicyCluster
    newSpec.InternalTrafficPolicy = &local
}
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Created `normalize.ServiceSpecDefaults` in `internal/common/normalize/service.go` that fills in `Type` (ClusterIP), `SessionAffinity` (None), and `InternalTrafficPolicy` (Cluster). Note: `IPFamilyPolicy` (SingleStack) mentioned in the review was NOT included in the implementation. Updated `EnsureService` in `deployment.go` to call `normalize.ServiceSpecDefaults` on both the new and existing specs before comparison (lines 112-114). Added tests in `service_test.go` verifying defaults and explicit-value preservation.

code_comments.body:
**CronJob/Job-spec-level API defaults not normalized — spurious updates on every reconcile**

`normalize.PodSpecDefaults` correctly handles pod-level defaults in the CronJob template, but `apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, *desiredSpec)` compares the full `CronJobSpec`, which includes several fields that the API server defaults on creation:

| Field | API-server default |
|---|---|
| `ConcurrencyPolicy` | `"Allow"` |
| `SuccessfulJobsHistoryLimit` | `&3` |
| `FailedJobsHistoryLimit` | `&1` |
| `JobTemplate.Spec.BackoffLimit` | `&6` |
| `JobTemplate.Spec.CompletionMode` | `"NonIndexed"` |

Callers that construct a `CronJob` without explicitly setting these common fields will see the stored `existing.Spec` with API defaults diverge from `desiredSpec` with zero/nil values on every reconcile. Unlike `RunJob`, this does not cause a delete-recreate cycle, but it triggers a `c.Update` call on every reconcile — generating continuous watch events and unnecessary operator churn.

Extend `normalize.PodSpecDefaults` or add a `normalize.CronJobSpecDefaults` that fills in these fields before comparison, following the same pattern used for `DeploymentSpec`.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: internal/common/job/job.go
Line: 110

Comment:
**CronJob/Job-spec-level API defaults not normalized — spurious updates on every reconcile**

`normalize.PodSpecDefaults` correctly handles pod-level defaults in the CronJob template, but `apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, *desiredSpec)` compares the full `CronJobSpec`, which includes several fields that the API server defaults on creation:

| Field | API-server default |
|---|---|
| `ConcurrencyPolicy` | `"Allow"` |
| `SuccessfulJobsHistoryLimit` | `&3` |
| `FailedJobsHistoryLimit` | `&1` |
| `JobTemplate.Spec.BackoffLimit` | `&6` |
| `JobTemplate.Spec.CompletionMode` | `"NonIndexed"` |

Callers that construct a `CronJob` without explicitly setting these common fields will see the stored `existing.Spec` with API defaults diverge from `desiredSpec` with zero/nil values on every reconcile. Unlike `RunJob`, this does not cause a delete-recreate cycle, but it triggers a `c.Update` call on every reconcile — generating continuous watch events and unnecessary operator churn.

Extend `normalize.PodSpecDefaults` or add a `normalize.CronJobSpecDefaults` that fills in these fields before comparison, following the same pattern used for `DeploymentSpec`.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Created `normalize.CronJobSpecDefaults` in `internal/common/normalize/cronjob.go` that fills in `ConcurrencyPolicy` (Allow), `SuccessfulJobsHistoryLimit` (3), `FailedJobsHistoryLimit` (1), and delegates to `JobSpecDefaults` for `BackoffLimit` (6) and `CompletionMode` (NonIndexed), plus `PodSpecDefaults` for the embedded PodSpec. Updated `EnsureCronJob` in `job.go` to normalize both desired and existing specs via `DeepCopy` before comparison (lines 110-113). Added comprehensive tests in `cronjob_test.go` covering `CronJobSpecDefaults`, `JobSpecDefaults`, and their explicit-value-preservation counterparts.

resolution_summary: All three review comments were addressed by creating dedicated normalize functions in a new `internal/common/normalize/` package — `DeploymentSpecDefaults`, `ServiceSpecDefaults`, `CronJobSpecDefaults`, and `JobSpecDefaults` — each filling in API-server defaults before spec comparison to prevent infinite update loops and spurious updates. The callers in `deployment.go` and `job.go` were updated to normalize both desired and existing specs via DeepCopy before comparison. Additionally, new files `normalize/deployment.go`, `normalize/service.go`, and `normalize/cronjob.go` were created with corresponding test files (`deployment_test.go`, `service_test.go`, `cronjob_test.go`) covering both default-filling and explicit-value-preservation scenarios.
